methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.docs: Must be `'status'`.

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: Callback receiving `{ action, status }` where `status.msgType` is a `RmxAudioStatusMessage`.

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName Must be `'status'`.

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc Callback receiving `{ action, status }` where `status.msgType` is a `RmxAudioStatusMessage`.

methods.docs: Subscribe to native playback status events (track changes, position, errors, etc.).

methods.name: setOptions
methods.docs:
Configure plugin behaviour (verbose logging, stream pause handling, notification icon).
Can be called at any time; not required before playback.

methods.name: initialize
methods.docs:
Initialise the native player, register status callbacks, and arm lock-screen / notification controls.
Call once before playback (e.g. on app start).

methods.name: release
methods.docs:
Tear down native resources (audio session, media service, observers).
Call when the app no longer needs background audio (e.g. on logout).

methods.name: setPlaylistItems
methods.docs:
Replace the entire playlist. Clears all previous items.
Use `options.retainPosition` to keep the current track and playback position.

methods.name: addItem
methods.docs: Append a single track to the end of the playlist.

methods.name: addAllItems
methods.docs:
Append multiple tracks to the end of the playlist.
Raises one `RMXSTATUS_ITEM_ADDED` event per track.

methods.name: removeItem
methods.docs:
Remove a track by index (preferred) or id.
If the removed track is currently playing, the next track starts automatically.

methods.name: removeItems
methods.docs:
Remove multiple tracks in a single batch.
If the currently playing track is removed, the next available track starts automatically.

methods.name: clearAllItems
methods.docs: Remove all tracks from the playlist. Raises `RMXSTATUS_PLAYLIST_CLEARED` and `RMXSTATUS_STOPPED`.

methods.name: getPlaylist
methods.docs: Return a snapshot of the current playlist items.

methods.name: play
methods.docs:
Start or resume playback of the current track.
No-op if the playlist is empty.

methods.name: pause
methods.docs: Pause playback of the current track.

methods.name: skipForward
methods.docs: Skip to the next track. At the end of the playlist, wraps to the beginning when loop is enabled.

methods.name: skipBack
methods.docs: Skip to the previous track. No-op when already at the first track.

methods.name: seekTo
methods.docs:
Seek to a position (seconds) in the currently playing track.
If the position exceeds track length, playback advances to the next track.

methods.name: playTrackByIndex
methods.docs: Jump to the track at the given 0-based index and start playback.

methods.name: playTrackById
methods.docs: Jump to the track with the given id and start playback.

methods.name: selectTrackByIndex
methods.docs: Select the track at the given index without necessarily starting playback.

methods.name: selectTrackById
methods.docs: Select the track with the given id without necessarily starting playback.

methods.name: setPlaybackVolume
methods.docs:
Set media stream volume. Float in range [0, 1].
Hardware volume controls still apply on top of this value.

methods.name: setLoop
methods.docs: When true, the playlist loops back to the first track after the last track completes.

methods.name: setPlaybackRate
methods.docs: Set playback speed. Float value; 0 pauses, 1 is normal speed.

methods.name: prepareForVideoHandoff
methods.docs:
Release native audio session / focus so a video player can own playback.

**Android:** pauses current track, abandons audio focus, stores head position. Does not stop the foreground media service.
**iOS:** pauses, captures head position, deactivates `AVAudioSession` with `notifyOthersOnDeactivation`.
**Web:** pauses HTMLAudioElement and stores `currentTime`.

Call immediately before native video starts (e.g. your video plugin's init method).

methods.name: resumeAfterVideoHandoff
methods.docs:
Re-arm native audio after video ends or, on Android, prewarm the media service before video starts.

**Without `prewarm` (typical exit path):**
- Android: when `play` is true (default), re-acquires focus and resumes at `position`. When `resumed` is `true`, JS should skip redundant `seekTo`/`play`. When `play` is false, clears handoff retain and returns `{ resumed: false }` so JS can seek without playing.
- iOS: restores pinned track, reactivates `AVAudioSession`, seeks to `position`, and when `play` is true starts playback (seek-then-play). Returns `{ resumed: true }` when native handled the handoff.
- Web: stores position only (no native session); returns `{ resumed: false }`.

**With `prewarm: true` (Android, before video):** starts `MediaService` in foreground at `position` but stays silent — no audio focus, no audible playback. Always returns `{ resumed: false }`.

methods.name: getLastKnownPosition
methods.docs:
Return the audio head position (seconds) captured during the most recent `prepareForVideoHandoff`
or passed to `resumeAfterVideoHandoff`.

interfaces.name: OnStatusCallbackData
interfaces.docs: Encapsulates the data received by an onStatus callback

properties.name: trackId
properties.docs:
The ID of this track. If the track is null or has completed, this value is "NONE"
If the playlist is completed, this value is "INVALID"

properties.name: msgType
properties.docs: The type of status update

properties.name: value
properties.docs:
The status payload. For all updates except ERROR, the data package is described by OnStatusCallbackUpdateData.
For Errors, the data is shaped as OnStatusErrorCallbackData

interfaces.name: OnStatusCallbackUpdateData
interfaces.docs: Contains the current track status as of the moment an onStatus update event is emitted.

properties.name: trackId
properties.docs:
The ID of this track corresponding to this event. If the track is null or has completed, this value is "NONE".
This will happen when skipping to the beginning or end of the playlist.
If the playlist is completed, this value is "INVALID"

properties.name: isStream
properties.docs: Boolean indicating whether this is a streaming track.

properties.name: currentIndex
properties.docs: The current index of the track in the playlist.

properties.name: status
properties.docs:
The current status of the track, as a string. This is used
to summarize the various event states that a track can be in; e.g. "playing" is true for any number
of track statuses. The Javascript interface takes care of this for you; this field is here only for reference.

properties.name: currentPosition
properties.docs: Current playback position of the reported track.

properties.name: duration
properties.docs: The known duration of the reported track. For streams or malformed MP3's, this value will be 0.

properties.name: playbackPercent
properties.docs: Progress of track playback, as a percent, in the range 0 - 100

properties.name: bufferPercent
properties.docs: Buffering progress of the track, as a percent, in the range 0 - 100

properties.name: bufferStart
properties.docs: The starting position of the buffering progress. For now, this is always reported as 0.

properties.name: bufferEnd
properties.docs:
The maximum position, in seconds, of the track buffer. For now, only the buffer with the maximum
playback position is reported, even if there are other segments (due to seeking, for example).
Practically speaking you don't need to worry about that, as in both implementations the
minor gaps are automatically filled in by the underlying players.

interfaces.name: OnStatusTrackChangedData
interfaces.docs:
Reports information about the playlist state when a track changes.
Includes the new track, its index, and the state of the playlist.

properties.name: currentItem
properties.docs:
The new track that has been selected. May be null if you are at the end of the playlist,
or the playlist has been emptied.

properties.name: currentIndex
properties.docs: The 0-based index of the new track. If the playlist has ended or been cleared, this will be -1.

properties.name: isAtEnd
properties.docs: Indicates whether the playlist is now currently at the last item in the list.

properties.name: isAtBeginning
properties.docs: Indicates whether the playlist is now at the first item in the list

properties.name: hasNext
properties.docs: Indicates if there are additional playlist items after the current item.

properties.name: hasPrevious
properties.docs: Indicates if there are any items before this one in the playlist.

interfaces.name: AudioTrack
interfaces.docs: An audio track for playback by the playlist.

properties.name: isStream
properties.docs:
This item is a streaming asset. Make sure this is set to true for stream URLs,
otherwise you will get odd behavior when the asset is paused.

properties.name: trackId
properties.docs: trackId is optional and if not passed in, an auto-generated UUID will be used.

properties.name: assetUrl
properties.docs:
URL of the asset; can be local, a URL, or a streaming URL.
If the asset is a stream, make sure that isStream is set to true,
otherwise the plugin can't properly handle the item's buffer.

properties.name: albumArt
properties.docs:
The local or remote URL to an image asset to be shown for this track.
If this is null, the plugin's default image is used.

properties.name: artist
properties.docs: The track's artist

properties.name: album
properties.docs: Album the track belongs to

properties.name: title
properties.docs: Title of the track

interfaces.name: OnStatusErrorCallbackData
interfaces.docs: Represents an error reported by the onStatus callback.

properties.name: code
properties.docs: Error code

properties.name: message
properties.docs: The error, as a message

interfaces.name: AudioPlayerOptions
interfaces.docs: Options governing the overall behavior of the audio player plugin

properties.name: verbose
properties.docs: Should the plugin's javascript dump the status message stream to the javascript console?

properties.name: resetStreamOnPause
properties.docs:
If true, when pausing a live stream, play will continue from the LIVE POSITION (e.g. the stream
jumps forward to the current point in time, rather than picking up where it left off when you paused).
If false, the stream will continue where you paused. The drawback of doing this is that when the audio
buffer fills, it will jump forward to the current point in time, cause a disjoint in playback.

Default is true.

properties.name: options
properties.docs: Further options for notifications

methods.name: toString
methods.docs: Returns a string representation of an array.

methods.name: toLocaleString
methods.docs: Returns a string representation of an array. The elements are converted to string using their toLocalString methods.

methods.name: pop
methods.docs:
Removes the last element from an array and returns it.
If the array is empty, undefined is returned and the array is not modified.

methods.name: push

parameters.name: items
parameters.docs: New elements to add to the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: items New elements to add to the array.

methods.docs: Appends new elements to the end of an array, and returns the new length of the array.

methods.name: concat

parameters.name: items
parameters.docs: Additional arrays and/or items to add to the end of the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: items Additional arrays and/or items to add to the end of the array.

methods.docs:
Combines two or more arrays.
This method returns a new array without modifying any existing arrays.

methods.name: concat

parameters.name: items
parameters.docs: Additional arrays and/or items to add to the end of the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: items Additional arrays and/or items to add to the end of the array.

methods.docs:
Combines two or more arrays.
This method returns a new array without modifying any existing arrays.

methods.name: join

parameters.name: separator
parameters.docs: A string used to separate one element of the array from the next in the resulting string. If omitted, the array elements are separated with a comma.

tags.name: param
tags.text: separator A string used to separate one element of the array from the next in the resulting string. If omitted, the array elements are separated with a comma.

methods.docs: Adds all the elements of an array into a string, separated by the specified separator string.

methods.name: reverse
methods.docs:
Reverses the elements in an array in place.
This method mutates the array and returns a reference to the same array.

methods.name: shift
methods.docs:
Removes the first element from an array and returns it.
If the array is empty, undefined is returned and the array is not modified.

methods.name: slice
methods.signature: (start?: number | undefined, end?: number | undefined) => T[]

parameters.name: start
parameters.docs:
The beginning index of the specified portion of the array.
If start is undefined, then the slice begins at index 0.

parameters.name: end
parameters.docs:
The end index of the specified portion of the array. This is exclusive of the element at the index 'end'.
If end is undefined, then the slice extends to the end of the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
start The beginning index of the specified portion of the array.
If start is undefined, then the slice begins at index 0.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
end The end index of the specified portion of the array. This is exclusive of the element at the index 'end'.
If end is undefined, then the slice extends to the end of the array.

methods.docs:
Returns a copy of a section of an array.
For both start and end, a negative index can be used to indicate an offset from the end of the array.
For example, -2 refers to the second to last element of the array.

methods.name: sort
methods.signature: (compareFn?: ((a: T, b: T) => number) | undefined) => this

parameters.name: compareFn
parameters.docs:
Function used to determine the order of the elements. It is expected to return
a negative value if first argument is less than second argument, zero if they're equal and a positive
value otherwise. If omitted, the elements are sorted in ascending, ASCII character order.
```ts
[11,2,22,1].sort((a, b) => a - b)
```

tags.name: param
tags.text:
compareFn Function used to determine the order of the elements. It is expected to return
a negative value if first argument is less than second argument, zero if they're equal and a positive
value otherwise. If omitted, the elements are sorted in ascending, ASCII character order.
```ts
[11,2,22,1].sort((a, b) => a - b)
```

methods.docs:
Sorts an array in place.
This method mutates the array and returns a reference to the same array.

methods.name: splice

parameters.name: start
parameters.docs: The zero-based location in the array from which to start removing elements.

parameters.name: deleteCount
parameters.docs: The number of elements to remove.

tags.name: param
tags.text: start The zero-based location in the array from which to start removing elements.

tags.name: param
tags.text: deleteCount The number of elements to remove.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: An array containing the elements that were deleted.

methods.docs: Removes elements from an array and, if necessary, inserts new elements in their place, returning the deleted elements.

methods.name: indexOf

parameters.name: searchElement
parameters.docs: The value to locate in the array.

parameters.name: fromIndex
parameters.docs: The array index at which to begin the search. If fromIndex is omitted, the search starts at index 0.

tags.name: param
tags.text: searchElement The value to locate in the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: fromIndex The array index at which to begin the search. If fromIndex is omitted, the search starts at index 0.

methods.docs: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a value in an array, or -1 if it is not present.

methods.name: lastIndexOf

parameters.name: searchElement
parameters.docs: The value to locate in the array.

parameters.name: fromIndex
parameters.docs: The array index at which to begin searching backward. If fromIndex is omitted, the search starts at the last index in the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: searchElement The value to locate in the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: fromIndex The array index at which to begin searching backward. If fromIndex is omitted, the search starts at the last index in the array.

methods.docs: Returns the index of the last occurrence of a specified value in an array, or -1 if it is not present.

methods.name: every
methods.signature: <S extends T>(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => value is S, thisArg?: any) => this is S[]

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.docs:
A function that accepts up to three arguments. The every method calls
the predicate function for each element in the array until the predicate returns a value
which is coercible to the Boolean value false, or until the end of the array.
parameters.type: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => value is S

parameters.name: thisArg
parameters.docs:
An object to which the this keyword can refer in the predicate function.
If thisArg is omitted, undefined is used as the this value.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
predicate A function that accepts up to three arguments. The every method calls
the predicate function for each element in the array until the predicate returns a value
which is coercible to the Boolean value false, or until the end of the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
thisArg An object to which the this keyword can refer in the predicate function.
If thisArg is omitted, undefined is used as the this value.

methods.docs: Determines whether all the members of an array satisfy the specified test.

the predicate function for each element in the array until the predicate returns a value
which is coercible to the Boolean value true, or until the end of the array.

parameters.name: thisArg

An object to which the this keyword can refer in the predicate function.
If thisArg is omitted, undefined is used as the this value.

tags.name: param
tags.text:
thisArg An object to which the this keyword can refer in the predicate function.
If thisArg is omitted, undefined is used as the this value.

methods.docs: Determines whether the specified callback function returns true for any element of an array.

parameters.type: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => void

parameters.name: thisArg

tags.name: param

methods.name: reduce
methods.signature: (callbackfn: (previousValue: T, currentValue: T, currentIndex: number, array: T[]) => T) => T

parameters.name: callbackfn
parameters.docs: A function that accepts up to four arguments. The reduce method calls the callbackfn function one time for each element in the array.
parameters.type: (previousValue: T, currentValue: T, currentIndex: number, array: T[]) => T

tags.name: param
tags.text: callbackfn A function that accepts up to four arguments. The reduce method calls the callbackfn function one time for each element in the array.

tags.name: param
tags.text: initialValue If initialValue is specified, it is used as the initial value to start the accumulation. The first call to the callbackfn function provides this value as an argument instead of an array value.

methods.docs: Calls the specified callback function for all the elements in an array. The return value of the callback function is the accumulated result, and is provided as an argument in the next call to the callback function.

properties.name: length
properties.docs: Gets or sets the length of the array. This is a number one higher than the highest index in the array.

methods.name: slice
methods.signature: (start?: number | undefined, end?: number | undefined) => T[]

interfaces.name: PlaylistItemOptions
interfaces.docs:
Options governing how the items are managed when using setPlaylistItems
to update the playlist. This is typically useful if you are retaining items
that were in the previous list.

properties.name: retainPosition
properties.docs:
If true, the plugin will continue playback from the current playback position after
setting the items to the playlist.

properties.name: playFromPosition
properties.docs:
If retainPosition is true, this value will tell the plugin the exact time to start from,
rather than letting the plugin decide based on current playback.

properties.name: playFromId
properties.docs:
If retainPosition is true, this value will tell the plugin the uid of the "current" item to start from,
rather than letting the plugin decide based on current playback.

properties.name: startPaused
properties.docs:
If playback should immediately begin when calling setPlaylistItems on the plugin.
Default is false;

properties.name: resumed
properties.docs:
`true` when native already handled seek (and play when requested) in place.
When `true`, JS should skip redundant `seekTo` / `play` to avoid a stutter.
`false` on web, prewarm, paused Android handoff, and Android last-resort `beginPlayback`.

properties.name: position
properties.docs: Resume position in seconds (video exit head or saved audio position).

properties.name: prewarm
properties.docs:
**Android only.** When `true`, promote `MediaService` to foreground and prepare at `position`
without requesting audio focus or playing audio. Use immediately after `prepareForVideoHandoff`
and before native video starts, while the app is still foregrounded.
Ignored on iOS (no-op). Not applicable on web.

properties.name: play
properties.docs:
When `true`, native starts audible playback after seeking to `position`.
When `false` (paused video exit), native must not start playback.
iOS defaults to `false` when omitted; Android defaults to `true` for legacy callers.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_NONE
members.docs:
The starting state of the plugin. You will never see this value;
it changes before the callbacks are even registered to report changes to this value.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_REGISTER
members.docs:
Raised when the plugin registers the callback handler for onStatus callbacks.
You will probably not be able to see this (nor do you need to).

members.name: RMXSTATUS_INIT
members.docs: Reserved for future use

members.name: RMXSTATUS_ERROR
members.docs: Indicates an error is reported in the 'value' field.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_LOADING
members.docs: The reported track is being loaded by the player

members.name: RMXSTATUS_CANPLAY
members.docs: The reported track is able to begin playback

members.name: RMXSTATUS_LOADED
members.docs: The reported track has loaded 100% of the file (either from disc or network)

members.name: RMXSTATUS_STALLED
members.docs: (iOS only): Playback has stalled due to insufficient network

members.name: RMXSTATUS_BUFFERING
members.docs: Reports an update in the reported track's buffering status

members.name: RMXSTATUS_PLAYING
members.docs: The reported track has started (or resumed) playing

members.name: RMXSTATUS_PAUSE
members.docs:
The reported track has been paused, either by the user or by the system.
(iOS only): This value is raised when MP3's are malformed (but still playable).
These require the user to explicitly press play again. This can be worked
around and is on the TODO list.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_PLAYBACK_POSITION
members.docs: Reports a change in the reported track's playback position.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_SEEK
members.docs:
The reported track has seeked.
On Android, only the plugin consumer can generate this (Notification controls on Android do not include a seek bar).
On iOS, the Command Center includes a seek bar so this will be reported when the user has seeked via Command Center.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_COMPLETED
members.docs: The reported track has completed playback.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_DURATION
members.docs:
The reported track's duration has changed. This is raised once, when duration is updated for the first time.
For streams, this value is never reported.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_STOPPED
members.docs: All playback has stopped, probably because the plugin is shutting down.

members.name: RMX_STATUS_SKIP_FORWARD
members.docs:
The playlist has skipped forward to the next track.
On both Android and iOS, this will be raised if the notification controls/Command Center were used to skip.
It is unlikely you need to consume this event: RMXSTATUS_TRACK_CHANGED is also reported when this occurs,
so you can generalize your track change handling in one place.

members.name: RMX_STATUS_SKIP_BACK
members.docs:
The playlist has skipped back to the previous track.
On both Android and iOS, this will be raised if the notification controls/Command Center were used to skip.
It is unlikely you need to consume this event: RMXSTATUS_TRACK_CHANGED is also reported when this occurs,
so you can generalize your track change handling in one place.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_TRACK_CHANGED
members.docs:
Reported when the current track has changed in the native player. This event contains full data about
the new track, including the index and the actual track itself. The type of the 'value' field in this case
is OnStatusTrackChangedData.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_PLAYLIST_COMPLETED
members.docs:
The entire playlist has completed playback.
After this event has been raised, the current item is set to null and the current index to -1.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_ITEM_ADDED
members.docs:
An item has been added to the playlist. For the setPlaylistItems and addAllItems methods, this status is
raised once for every track in the collection.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_ITEM_REMOVED
members.docs:
An item has been removed from the playlist. For the removeItems and clearAllItems methods, this status is
raised once for every track that was removed.

members.name: RMXSTATUS_PLAYLIST_CLEARED
members.docs: All items have been removed from the playlist

members.name: RMXSTATUS_VIEWDISAPPEAR
members.docs: Just for testing.. you don't need this and in fact can never receive it, the plugin is destroyed before it can be raised.
